 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→    ) {
118→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119→        if (autoCorrect) {
120→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
127→}

 8→/**
 9→ * A representation of a block of code. Use one of the factory methods [fromFile], [fromPath], [fromSnippet] or [fromStdin] to instantiate.
10→ */
11→public class Code private constructor(
12→    public val content: String,
13→    public val fileName: String?,
14→    public val filePath: Path?,
15→    public val script: Boolean,
16→    public val isStdIn: Boolean,
17→) {
18→    public companion object {
19→        /**
20→         * Create [Code] from a [file] containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are taken
21→         * into account.
22→         */
23→        public fun fromFile(file: File): Code =
24→            Code(
25→                content = file.readText(),
26→                fileName = file.name,
27→                filePath = file.toPath(),
28→                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
29→                isStdIn = false,
30→            )
31→
32→        /**
33→         * Create [Code] from a [path] to a file containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are
34→         * taken into account. This method is intended to be used in unit tests. In order to work with the Ktlint test file system it needs
35→         * to make additional call to get the file system which makes it slower compared to [fromFile]. Prefer to use [fromFile].
36→         */
37→        public fun fromPath(path: Path): Code {
38→            // Resolve the file based on the file system of the original path given.
39→            val file =
40→                path
41→                    .fileSystem
42→                    .file(path.pathString)
43→            return Code(
44→                content = file.readStrings().joinToString(separator = "\n"),
45→                fileName = file.name,
46→                filePath = path,
47→                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
48→                isStdIn = false,
49→            )
50→        }
51→
52→        /**
53→         * The [content] represent a valid piece of Kotlin code or Kotlin script. The '.editorconfig' files on the filesystem are ignored as
54→         * the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the same time respecting the
55→         * '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
56→         */
57→        public fun fromSnippet(
58→            content: String,
59→            script: Boolean = false,
60→        ): Code =
61→            Code(
62→                content = content,
63→                filePath = null,
64→                fileName = null,
65→                script = script,
66→                isStdIn = true,
67→            )
68→
69→        /**
70→         * Create [Code] by reading the snippet from 'stdin'. No '.editorconfig' are taken into account.  The '.editorconfig' files on the
71→         * filesystem are ignored as the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the
72→         * same time respecting the '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
73→         */
74→        public fun fromStdin(): Code = fromSnippet(String(System.`in`.readBytes()))
75→    }
76→}

 86→/**
 87→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 88→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 89→ *
 90→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 91→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 92→ * indentation rule is not run.
 93→ */
 94→public class WrappingRule :
 95→    StandardRule(
 96→        id = "wrapping",
 97→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 98→            setOf(
 99→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
100→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
101→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
102→            ),
103→        visitorModifiers =
104→            setOf(
105→                RunAfterRule(
106→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
107→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
108→                ),
109→            ),
110→    ) {
111→    private var line = 1
112→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
113→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
114→
115→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
116→        line = 1
117→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
118→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
119→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
120→        )
121→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
122→    }
123→
124→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        when (node.elementType) {
130→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
131→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
132→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
133→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
134→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
135→//            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeParameterList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
138→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
143→        node: ASTNode,
144→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146→    ) {
147→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
148→
149→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
150→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
151→            return
152→        }
153→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
154→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
155→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
156→        val wrapBlock =
157→            when {
158→                startOfBlock.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) -> {
159→                    // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
160→                    false
161→                }
162→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
163→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
164→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
165→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
166→                }
167→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
168→                    val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
169→                        node
170→                            .leaves(false)
171→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
173→                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
174→                        node
175→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
177→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
178→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
179→                    lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength
180→                }
181→                else -> false
182→            }
183→        if (wrapBlock) {
184→            startOfBlock
185→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
186→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
187→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
188→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
189→                        autoCorrect,
190→                        emit,
191→                    )
192→                }
193→        }
194→    }

content.text:
The issue:  
You want a block comment inside a single-line block to be ignored for wrapping, specifically for code like `val foo = { /* no-op */ }`, so that such code remains unchanged (the comment should not be replaced with an end-of-line comment). The current logic in `CommentWrappingRule` seems to convert single-line block comments after code into EOL comments, which is incorrect for single-line block comments *inside* a lambda/block (e.g. function literals).

**Analysis:**  
- The relevant logic is in `CommentWrappingRule`, especially the method `precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine` where it matches single-line block comments after code elements and replaces them.
- The problem:  
  There is no check for whether the block comment is inside a block (e.g. inside a lambda like `{ /* no-op */ }`).  
  In such cases, it shouldn't autocorrect the block comment.

**Proper fix:**  
- Update the logic in `precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine` so it does *not* autocorrect single-line block comments (e.g., `/* no-op */`) that are the only contents inside a single-line block.

**How to implement:**
1. In `precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine`, before autocorrecting, check if the block comment is inside a single-line block (e.g., inside `{}` for lambdas or similar constructs).
2. If it's the only leaf in that block, ignore it (do not emit a violation or change).
3. Otherwise, keep existing behavior.

**Minimal targeted code change plan:**
- Update the `precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine` method (and possibly the main logic in `beforeVisitChildNodes`) in `CommentWrappingRule.kt` to check for this case.
- The test suite already verifies this ("should be kept unchanged"), so the change should work correctly.

Proceeding to implement.

 18→/**
 19→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 20→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 21→ */
 22→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 23→    StandardRule(
 24→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 25→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 26→            setOf(
 27→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 28→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 29→            ),
 30→    ) {
 31→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 32→        node: ASTNode,
 33→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 34→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 35→    ) {
 36→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 37→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 38→                node
 39→                    .prevLeaf()
 40→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 41→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 42→                node
 43→                    .nextLeaf()
 44→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 45→
 46→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 47→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 48→            ) {
 49→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 50→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 51→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 52→                    //    some comment
 53→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 54→                    emit(
 55→                        node.startOffset,
 56→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 57→                            "disallowed",
 58→                        false,
 59→                    )
 60→                } else {
 61→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 62→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 63→                    emit(
 64→                        node.startOffset,
 65→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 66→                        false,
 67→                    )
 68→                }
 69→                return
 70→            }
 71→
 72→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 73→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 74→
 75→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 76→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 77→        }
 78→    }

 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 84→    ) {
 85→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 86→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 87→            emit(
 88→                startOffset,
 89→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 90→                true,
 91→            )
 92→            if (autoCorrect) {
 93→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 94→            }
 95→        } else {
 96→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 97→            emit(
 98→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
 99→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
100→                false,
101→            )
102→        }
103→    }

105→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {

 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→    ) {
118→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119→        if (autoCorrect) {
120→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
127→}

17→) {
18→    public companion object {
19→        /**
20→         * Create [Code] from a [file] containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are taken
21→         * into account.
22→         */
23→        public fun fromFile(file: File): Code =
24→            Code(
25→                content = file.readText(),
26→                fileName = file.name,
27→                filePath = file.toPath(),
28→                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
29→                isStdIn = false,
30→            )
31→
32→        /**
33→         * Create [Code] from a [path] to a file containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are
34→         * taken into account. This method is intended to be used in unit tests. In order to work with the Ktlint test file system it needs
35→         * to make additional call to get the file system which makes it slower compared to [fromFile]. Prefer to use [fromFile].
36→         */
37→        public fun fromPath(path: Path): Code {
38→            // Resolve the file based on the file system of the original path given.
39→            val file =
40→                path
41→                    .fileSystem
42→                    .file(path.pathString)
43→            return Code(
44→                content = file.readStrings().joinToString(separator = "\n"),
45→                fileName = file.name,
46→                filePath = path,
47→                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
48→                isStdIn = false,
49→            )
50→        }
51→
52→        /**
53→         * The [content] represent a valid piece of Kotlin code or Kotlin script. The '.editorconfig' files on the filesystem are ignored as
54→         * the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the same time respecting the
55→         * '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
56→         */
57→        public fun fromSnippet(
58→            content: String,
59→            script: Boolean = false,
60→        ): Code =
61→            Code(
62→                content = content,
63→                filePath = null,
64→                fileName = null,
65→                script = script,
66→                isStdIn = true,
67→            )
68→
69→        /**
70→         * Create [Code] by reading the snippet from 'stdin'. No '.editorconfig' are taken into account.  The '.editorconfig' files on the
71→         * filesystem are ignored as the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the
72→         * same time respecting the '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
73→         */
74→        public fun fromStdin(): Code = fromSnippet(String(System.`in`.readBytes()))
75→    }

 86→/**
 87→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 88→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 89→ *
 90→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 91→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 92→ * indentation rule is not run.
 93→ */
 94→public class WrappingRule :
 95→    StandardRule(
 96→        id = "wrapping",
 97→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 98→            setOf(
 99→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
100→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
101→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
102→            ),
103→        visitorModifiers =
104→            setOf(
105→                RunAfterRule(
106→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
107→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
108→                ),
109→            ),
110→    ) {
111→    private var line = 1
112→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
113→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
114→
115→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
116→        line = 1
117→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
118→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
119→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
120→        )
121→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
122→    }
123→
124→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        when (node.elementType) {
130→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
131→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
132→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
133→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
134→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
135→//            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeParameterList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)

142→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(

144→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146→    ) {
147→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
148→
149→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
150→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
151→            return
152→        }
153→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
154→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
155→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
156→        val wrapBlock =
157→            when {
158→                startOfBlock.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) -> {
159→                    // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
160→                    false
161→                }
162→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
163→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
164→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
165→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
166→                }
167→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
168→                    val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
169→                        node
170→                            .leaves(false)
171→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
173→                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
174→                        node
175→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
177→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
178→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
179→                    lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength
180→                }
181→                else -> false
182→            }
183→        if (wrapBlock) {
184→            startOfBlock
185→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
186→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
187→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
188→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
189→                        autoCorrect,
190→                        emit,
191→                    )
192→                }
193→        }
194→    }

 18→/**
 19→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 20→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 21→ */
 22→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 23→    StandardRule(
 24→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 25→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 26→            setOf(
 27→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 28→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 29→            ),
 30→    ) {
 31→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 32→        node: ASTNode,
 33→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 34→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 35→    ) {
 36→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 37→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 38→                node
 39→                    .prevLeaf()
 40→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 41→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 42→                node
 43→                    .nextLeaf()
 44→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 45→
 46→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 47→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 48→            ) {
 49→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 50→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 51→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 52→                    //    some comment
 53→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 54→                    emit(
 55→                        node.startOffset,
 56→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 57→                            "disallowed",
 58→                        false,
 59→                    )
 60→                } else {
 61→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 62→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 63→                    emit(
 64→                        node.startOffset,
 65→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 66→                        false,
 67→                    )
 68→                }
 69→                return
 70→            }
 71→
 72→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 73→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 74→
 75→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 76→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 81→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 84→    ) {
 85→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 86→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 87→            emit(
 88→                startOffset,
 89→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 90→                true,
 91→            )
 92→            if (autoCorrect) {
 93→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 94→            }
 95→        } else {
 96→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 97→            emit(
 98→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
 99→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
100→                false,
101→            )
102→        }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
106→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
107→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
108→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
109→        rawRemove()
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
113→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
114→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
115→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
116→    ) {
117→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
118→        if (autoCorrect) {
119→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
124→
125→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
126→}
